free_text: Mass yields in the fission of uranium-235 and
plutonium-239 in the neutron spectrum of a gas-cooled
fast reactor
- final publ.
- prelim.data
- prelim.data
PROTEUS fast reactor, Wuerenlingen.
Gas-cooled fast reactor.
Gas-cooled fast reactor spectrum with a flux
of 3 10*9 n/cm*2/sec. A graph of the spectrum is
shown with a broad peak from 0.01 MeV to 1.0 MeV.
The median energy is quoted as 180 keV.
Several 40 mg samples of uranium dioxide, enriched
to 97.5% U-235, and plutonium dioxide, enriched
Irradiation were performed in the fast zone of the
PROTEUS reactor. The uranium and plutonium samples
were irradiated for 1 and 2 hours, respectively.
Gamma-ray spectroscopy of fission products
after chemical separation of fission products in
aqueous solution. Only zirconium, niobium, tellurium
and iodine were partially separated, all other
products were fully extracted.
A 40 cm*3 Ge(Li) spectrometer, resolution
of 2.0 keV at 1.3 MeV. Counting started 80 min after
the irradiation and continued for 10 days.
Relative yields are converted to absolute yields by
normalizing the total fission yields of all nuclides
in the heavy peak to 100%. The measured yields
amount to 66% of the total yields in the heavy peak,
yields of missing mass chains were interpolated
An error of 10 to 20% was assigned to the
interpolated yields.
SD: Entry revised.
SD: Correction in Subent 002.
Relative yields were converted to absolute yields by
normalizing the total fission yields of all nuclides in
the heavy peak to 100%. The measured yields amount to
~66% of the total yield in the heavy peak. The yields
of the missing mass chains were interpolated.

compared with data from this ref.

In Tbl.I result was assigned to Ba, Ce but in
Tbl.III value compared with estimated Ce from
F.L.Lisman+ and Ce from J.G.Cuninghame+
One standard deviation, including
uncertainties in analysis of absolute yields and

SD: REACTION code was modified.
STATUS updated. Col. ELEMENT added.
SD: EN-MEAN-NM and EN-DUM-DN added.
Relative yields were converted to absolute yields by
normalizing the total fission yields of all nuclides in
the heavy peak to 100%. The measured yields amount to
~66% of the total yield in the heavy peak. The yields
of the missing mass chains were interpolated.
